repo_notes.content: SCOPE: Generate EXACTLY three or four English-only pages: 'Skill Mapping', 'Technical Evidence', 'Quantifiables', and conditionally 'Dependency Graph' (required for repos with inter-process communication; omitted for single-process libraries — see Dependency Graph page notes). Zero Chinese text. All pages serve as human-readable documentation AND as structured input for an automated extraction pipeline. Do NOT generate resume bullets, accomplishment statements, or any text intended for direct inclusion in a resume. Your job is EVIDENCE EXTRACTION, not resume writing.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: TAXONOMY FILE: The file .devin/taxonomy.yaml in this repo defines 8 tracks (T1-T8), each with 5 levels (L0-L4). Each level contains a 'skills' map of snake_case skill keys, each with an 'aliases' array. When mapping technologies to skills, use ONLY (taxonomy_ref, skill_name) pairs that exist in this file. Format: taxonomy_ref = 'T{1-8}-L{0-4}', skill_name = a key under that level's skills map. If a technology has no match, list it separately as an unmapped skill with a suggested track and level.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: STRUCTURED DATA PREFERENCE: Each page MUST contain ONE primary fenced YAML code block (```yaml ... ```) as its structured payload. All supplementary discussion should be OUTSIDE the code block as prose. The YAML block must be parseable by a strict YAML parser — no trailing commas, no unquoted special characters, no tab indentation. If a string contains colons or special chars, wrap in single quotes.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: EVIDENCE RULES: Every claim must cite at least one source file path and approximate line range. Format: {file: 'path/to/file', lines: '42-87', summary: '≤20 words'}. Classify evidence as: 'implemented' (actual usage in source code — function definitions, algorithm implementations, API calls), 'configured' (launch files, config files, parameter tuning), 'declared' (package.json, CMakeLists.txt, requirements.txt dependency only), or 'tested' (appears in test files). Only 'implemented' and 'configured' count as proficiency evidence. 'declared' is noted but flagged. If line numbers are uncertain, prefix with '~'. If path is inferred, append '[inferred]'.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: ATS KEYWORD SOURCING: The 'aliases' arrays in .devin/taxonomy.yaml are the PRIMARY source for exact ATS keywords. Separate keywords into: 'exact' (drawn verbatim from taxonomy aliases) and 'semantic' (additional terms observed in code — class names, variable names, API calls, ROS topic names, message types — that are NOT already in the aliases array). Do NOT duplicate taxonomy aliases into semantic. Semantic keywords must include the source_file where they were observed.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: QUANTIFICATION RULES: When extracting numbers, always include: (1) the raw value, (2) the unit, (3) the source file/line, (4) whether the number is 'measured' (from benchmarks, tests, logs), 'configured' (from config/params), 'counted' (from code structure like file count, test count), or 'claimed' (from README/docs with no code/test evidence — always tag '[unverified]'). Mark any derived or estimated values with '[est.]' and show the derivation. Prefer concrete numbers over vague qualifiers.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content:
USAGE DEPTH CALIBRATION: The usage_depth enum has strict definitions and imposes a CEILING on claimable taxonomy levels:
- 'declared': Appears only in dependency manifests (package.xml, requirements.txt, CMakeLists find_package). Ceiling: L0.
- 'imported': Import/include statements exist and the library is called, but only via default API with no customization. Ceiling: L1.
- 'configured': Parameters tuned, launch files customized, config files authored for this specific use case. Ceiling: L2.
- 'customized': Source-level modifications — subclassed, extended, wrote custom plugins, non-trivial algorithmic usage. Ceiling: L3.
- 'architected': Designed the system around this technology — defined interfaces, made architectural trade-offs, wrote abstractions others consume. Ceiling: L4.
If the taxonomy_ref level EXCEEDS the usage_depth ceiling, this is a FLAG — Stage 2 will reject the claim. When in doubt, classify DOWN, not up.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: CROSS-PAGE REFERENCES: Every skill in Skill Mapping MUST reference at least one evidence_unit_ref (from Technical Evidence) and SHOULD reference metric_refs (from Quantifiables) when applicable. Every evidence unit MUST reference at least one quantifiable_ref if its statement involves any magnitude, frequency, count, or performance claim. IDs must be consistent across pages — E01 in Skill Mapping must be the same E01 in Technical Evidence, M01 in Skill Mapping must be the same M01 in Quantifiables. Stage 2 will REJECT pages with dangling references or inconsistent IDs.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL: (1) If you cannot locate a file path with confidence, DO NOT GUESS — use '[not found]' as the path and add '[inferred]' tag, and set classification to 'declared'. Stage 2 will attempt to resolve. (2) If line numbers are uncertain beyond ±20 lines, use '~' prefix AND set a 'confidence: low' field. (3) If you are unsure whether usage is 'configured' vs 'customized', ALWAYS choose the LOWER depth. (4) NEVER round metrics to clean numbers (e.g., do not turn 47.3 into '~50'). Extract the EXACT value from source. (5) If a README claims performance numbers but no code/test evidence supports them, extract with source_type: 'claimed' and flag: '[unverified]'. (6) If the repo contains fewer than 5 extractable metrics, that is FINE — do not pad with estimated values. Quality over quantity.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: PROVENANCE METADATA: Every page's YAML block MUST begin with a '_meta' section documenting scan coverage: files_scanned, files_in_repo, scan_coverage_pct, directories_skipped (e.g., vendored/third-party code), extraction_confidence (high|medium|low), confidence_rationale, and known_gaps (capabilities you suspect exist but could not fully analyze). Stage 2 uses this to decide whether to request a second-pass extraction.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

pages.title: Skill Mapping
pages.purpose: Identify ALL technologies, frameworks, libraries, algorithms, and design patterns in this codebase. Map each to the robotics skill taxonomy in .devin/taxonomy.yaml using (taxonomy_ref, skill_name) pairs. For each skill, assess usage depth (with ceiling check), provide file-level evidence, extract ATS keywords, and document typed relationships to other skills in the map. Also document the tech stack (languages with approximate LOC%, frameworks, tools). List any technologies not covered by the taxonomy as unmapped skills with a suggested (track, level) placement.

page_notes.content:
PREFERRED YAML STRUCTURE:
```
schema_version: 3
source_repo: '<owner/repo>'
generated_at: '<ISO-8601>'
_meta:
  files_scanned: <int>
  files_in_repo: <int>
  scan_coverage_pct: <float>
  directories_skipped: ['<path>', ...]
  extraction_confidence: <high|medium|low>
  confidence_rationale: '<1 sentence>'
  known_gaps: ['<description>']
skills:
  - taxonomy_ref: 'T{n}-L{n}'
    skill_name: '<snake_case from taxonomy>'
    usage_depth: <declared|imported|configured|customized|architected>
    usage_depth_rationale:
      classification_trigger: '<what specific code pattern justifies this depth>'
      ceiling_check: '<T{n}-L{n} vs depth ceiling → PASS|FAIL>'
      strongest_evidence_id: '<E{nn}>'
    file_count: <int>
    proficiency_evidence:
      - file: '<path>'
        lines: '<start-end>'
        classification: <implemented|configured|declared|tested>
        summary: '<≤30 words>'
        confidence: <high|medium|low>
    ats_keywords:
      exact: ['<from taxonomy aliases>']
      semantic:
        - keyword: '<observed term>'
          source_file: '<path where observed>'
    related_skills:
      - target: '<taxonomy_ref:skill_name>'
        relationship_type: <co_implementation|pipeline_stage|dependency|interface_boundary|runtime_composition>
        integration_evidence:
          file: '<path>'
          lines: '<range>'
          summary: '<≤30 words showing the two skills interacting>'
        integration_pattern: '<≤30 words: how these two skills interact architecturally>'
        co_occurrence_files: <int>
    evidence_unit_refs: ['<E{nn}>', ...]
    metric_refs: ['<M{nn}>', ...]
tech_stack:
  languages:
    - name: '<lang>'
      version: '<if detectable>'
      loc_pct: <float>
  frameworks: [{name, version, role}]
  build_tools: [{name, version}]
  testing: [{framework, test_count_est}]
unmapped_skills:
  - name: '<technology>'
    observed_in: '<file>'
    suggested_track: 'T{n}'
    suggested_level: 'L{n}'
    rationale: '<why this track/level>'
```
After the YAML block, add prose sections: '## Architecture Context' (how components fit together) and '## Skill Relationship Graph' (which skills are used together, what the integration pattern is, and which files demonstrate the coupling — this is critical for the downstream bullet-composition stage).

RELATIONSHIP QUALITY: related_skills edges are the PRIMARY input for cross-track resume bullets. A flat list of taxonomy_refs is INSUFFICIENT. Each relationship MUST include: (1) the relationship_type enum value, (2) at least one file-level citation showing the two skills interacting in the SAME code region (not just the same repo), (3) a ≤25-word integration_pattern describing the architectural coupling. Relationship types map to bullet clause structures: co_implementation → 'Implemented X with Y', pipeline_stage → 'X feeding into Y', dependency → 'X built on Y', interface_boundary → 'X communicating with Y via Z', runtime_composition → 'X orchestrating Y at runtime'. If two skills appear in the same repo but never interact in the same file or call chain, they are NOT related — do not list them.

pages.title: Technical Evidence
pages.purpose: Document the architectural decisions, design patterns, algorithms, integration patterns, and engineering challenges evident in this codebase. Do NOT write resume bullets. Instead, extract factual 'evidence units' — atomic statements about what the code does, how, and why — each with file-level citations. Group evidence by the taxonomy tracks they relate to. Also extract the project's purpose, problem domain, and deployment context.

page_notes.content:
PREFERRED YAML STRUCTURE:
```
schema_version: 3
source_repo: '<owner/repo>'
_meta:
  files_scanned: <int>
  files_in_repo: <int>
  scan_coverage_pct: <float>
  directories_skipped: ['<path>', ...]
  extraction_confidence: <high|medium|low>
  confidence_rationale: '<1 sentence>'
  known_gaps: ['<description>']
project:
  title: '<descriptive project name>'
  domain: '<e.g. mobile manipulation, autonomous navigation>'
  purpose: '<2-3 sentences: what problem does this solve>'
  deployment_context: '<simulation|hardware|both, what platform>'
evidence_units:
  - id: 'E01'
    category: <architecture|algorithm|integration|optimization|testing|deployment>
    statement: '<1-2 factual sentences about what the code does — NOT a resume bullet>'
    taxonomy_refs: ['<T{n}-L{n}:skill_name>']
    evidence:
      - file: '<path>'
        lines: '<range>'
        classification: <implemented|configured|declared|tested>
        summary: '<≤30 words>'
        confidence: <high|medium|low>
    complexity_indicators:
      - type: <algorithmic|systems|scale|realtime|safety>
        detail: '<what makes this non-trivial>'
    integration_context: '<what other components/skills does this connect to>'
    quantifiable_refs: ['<M{nn}>', ...]
    skill_refs: ['<T{n}-L{n}:skill_name>', ...]
architectural_decisions:
  - decision: '<what was decided>'
    alternatives_visible: '<evidence of other approaches considered, if any>'
    evidence_file: '<path>'
    related_evidence_units: ['E01', ...]
```
After the YAML block, add: '## Design Pattern Analysis' and '## Engineering Challenges' (inferred from code complexity, error handling, edge cases). These prose sections give the downstream pipeline context for composing XYZ-decomposed bullets.

EVIDENCE UNIT QUALITY: (1) Statements must be FACTUAL and VERIFIABLE from the cited files — no speculation about performance or impact. (2) Each evidence unit should be ATOMIC — one architectural fact, not a compound sentence. (3) Aim for 10-20 evidence units per repo (more for larger repos, but do NOT pad with trivial units). (4) Complexity indicators are critical: the downstream pipeline uses these to assess which evidence units warrant L3-L4 skill claims vs L1-L2. (5) IDs sequential: E01-E99. (6) Every evidence unit with ANY quantitative claim MUST have a quantifiable_refs link to the Quantifiables page — if the number is not extracted there, it cannot appear here.

pages.title: Quantifiables
pages.purpose: Extract ALL numbers, metrics, and quantifiable signals from this codebase. This includes: configured parameters (control gains, rates, tolerances), code-structure counts (files, functions, test cases, LOC per module), performance-related values (frequencies, latencies, buffer sizes), and any benchmark or test results visible in the repo. Do NOT interpret these numbers — just extract them with source evidence. The downstream pipeline decides which numbers become resume metrics.

page_notes.content:
PREFERRED YAML STRUCTURE:
```
schema_version: 3
source_repo: '<owner/repo>'
_meta:
  files_scanned: <int>
  files_in_repo: <int>
  scan_coverage_pct: <float>
  directories_skipped: ['<path>', ...]
  extraction_confidence: <high|medium|low>
  confidence_rationale: '<1 sentence>'
  known_gaps: ['<description>']
metrics:
  - id: 'M01'
    category: <performance|scale|reliability|complexity|configuration|test_coverage>
    raw_value: <number or range>
    unit: '<e.g. Hz, ms, %, count, DOF, lines>'
    source_type: <measured|configured|counted|estimated|claimed>
    source:
      file: '<path>'
      lines: '<range>'
      context: '<≤15 words: where this number appears>'
      confidence: <high|medium|low>
    taxonomy_refs: ['<T{n}-L{n}:skill_name if applicable>']
    evidence_unit_refs: ['<E{nn}>']
    resume_potential: <high|medium|low>
    resume_potential_rationale: '<why this number could/couldn't anchor a resume metric>'
code_structure:
  total_loc: <int>
  modules:
    - name: '<directory or package>'
      loc: <int>
      file_count: <int>
      primary_language: '<lang>'
      test_file_count: <int>
  dependency_count: <int>
  ros_nodes: <int, if applicable>
  ros_topics: <int, if applicable>
  launch_configurations: <int, if applicable>
derived_metrics:
  - id: 'D01'
    description: '<what was calculated>'
    value: <number>
    unit: '<unit>'
    derivation: '<formula or logic used>'
    source_metrics: ['M01', ...]
    confidence: <high|medium|low>
```
After the YAML block, add: '## Metric Interpretation Notes' (domain context for what these numbers mean in robotics — e.g., '100Hz control loop is standard for manipulators, 1kHz is high-performance').

EXTRACTION COMPLETENESS: Search these locations exhaustively: (1) Config/param files (YAML, JSON, XML, launch files) for configured values. (2) Constants and #define in source for hardcoded parameters. (3) Test files for expected values and tolerances. (4) README/docs for claimed performance — use source_type: 'claimed' and tag '[unverified]' if no code evidence. (5) CI/build configs for tool versions and flags. (6) ROS parameter servers, dynamic_reconfigure definitions. Every number must have a source citation. Aim for 15-40 metrics per repo, but if the repo yields fewer, do NOT invent metrics to reach the target.

pages.title: Dependency Graph
pages.purpose: Extract the runtime communication architecture of this system. For ROS/ROS2 repos: map all nodes, topics (with message types), services, action servers, TF frames, and launch-file composition. For non-ROS repos: map all inter-process communication, API boundaries, event buses, shared-memory interfaces, and plugin architectures. This page captures SYSTEM-LEVEL DESIGN — how components are composed into a working pipeline. Do NOT interpret or evaluate the architecture; extract it factually with file citations. CONDITIONAL: If this repo has NO inter-process communication and is a single-process library, SKIP this page entirely and note in the Skill Mapping prose: 'Dependency Graph page omitted: single-process library with no IPC architecture.'

page_notes.content:
PREFERRED YAML STRUCTURE:
```
schema_version: 3
source_repo: '<owner/repo>'
_meta:
  files_scanned: <int>
  files_in_repo: <int>
  scan_coverage_pct: <float>
  directories_skipped: ['<path>', ...]
  extraction_confidence: <high|medium|low>
  confidence_rationale: '<1 sentence>'
  known_gaps: ['<description>']
architecture_type: <ros2|ros1|microservice|monolith|plugin_based|hybrid>
nodes:
  - name: '<node_name>'
    source_file: '<path to main file>'
    lifecycle_managed: <true|false>
    publishes:
      - topic: '<topic_name>'
        msg_type: '<package/MsgType>'
        frequency_hz: <number|null>
        frequency_source: {file: '<path>', lines: '<range>'}
        qos_profile: '<if specified>'
    subscribes:
      - topic: '<topic_name>'
        msg_type: '<package/MsgType>'
        callback_file: '<path>'
        callback_lines: '<range>'
    services:
      - name: '<service_name>'
        srv_type: '<package/SrvType>'
        role: <server|client>
        source: {file: '<path>', lines: '<range>'}
    action_servers:
      - name: '<action_name>'
        action_type: '<package/ActionType>'
        role: <server|client>
        supports_preemption: <true|false|unknown>
        source: {file: '<path>', lines: '<range>'}
    parameters:
      - name: '<param_name>'
        type: '<data_type>'
        default: '<value>'
        dynamic_reconfigure: <true|false>
        source: {file: '<path>', lines: '<range>'}
tf_tree:
  frames:
    - parent: '<frame_id>'
      child: '<frame_id>'
      publisher_node: '<node_name>'
      static: <true|false>
launch_composition:
  - launch_file: '<path>'
    nodes_launched: ['<node_name>', ...]
    includes: ['<other launch file>', ...]
    conditionals: ['<brief description of conditional logic>']
pipeline_chains:
  - name: '<descriptive name, e.g. perception_to_control>'
    chain: ['<node>→<topic>→<node>→<topic>→<node>']
    latency_budget_ms: <number|null>
    latency_source: {file: '<path>', lines: '<range>'}
    taxonomy_refs_involved: ['<T{n}-L{n}:skill_name>', ...]
    evidence_unit_refs: ['<E{nn}>', ...]
```
After the YAML block, add: '## System Architecture Narrative' (2-3 paragraphs describing the overall data flow) and '## Concurrency & Real-Time Design' (threading model, executor type, callback groups, priority settings — with file citations).

PIPELINE CHAINS ARE CRITICAL: The pipeline_chains section captures end-to-end data flow paths through the system. Stage 3 uses these to compose 'system-level' resume bullets that demonstrate architectural ownership. Each chain should trace the full path from sensor input to actuator output (or the relevant subset). The taxonomy_refs_involved field enables Stage 3 to generate cross-track bullets like 'Designed a perception-to-control pipeline spanning [list of skills]'. For non-ROS repos, adapt the chain concept to whatever IPC mechanism exists (HTTP call chains, message queue flows, shared-memory pipelines).
